assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.
The issue is limited to foreign currency risk on an intragroup balance or transaction related to dividends.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposures to changes in fair value of recognised assets or liabilities attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidation.
